caption:
Stop writing useMemo and useCallback everywhere.

React Compiler does it for you. Automatically. At build time.

Your hand-written memoization is now dead code.

Which approach are you using? A or B?

#react #reactjs #javascript #webdev #coding #programming #frontend #developer #nextjs #codingtips

a.label: Manual memoization

b.label: React Compiler

slides.title: B wins. Same performance. Zero boilerplate.

verdicts.title: Manual memo is noisy and error-prone
verdicts.text: useMemo and useCallback everywhere. Dependency arrays that break silently. 40% of your component is optimization boilerplate.

verdicts.title: React Compiler memoizes at build time
verdicts.text: Write plain React. The compiler analyzes your code and inserts memoization automatically. Same output. Zero manual work.

slides.takeaway: The compiler doesn't just memoize — it understands your intent better than you do.

slides.title: What the compiler actually does.
slides.sub: It rewrites your components at build time. You never see the optimization.

items.text: No useMemo, useCallback, or React.memo needed
Works at build time — zero runtime cost
Already used in production at Meta

slides.teaser: Next: React Server Components — the pattern that killed API routes
